Document:
# Space Stories - Marine Corps Game Rules
The rules in this section and the in-game guide are primary and take priority over all other sources, including Discord and the Wiki.
## Recent Changes (28.10.2025) - [textlink="Rule 2: Roleplay" link="STRolePlay"] has been reworked and expanded — new sections added about IC communication, toxicity, awards, and Xenomorph behavior. This rule now establishes overall roleplay standards for all players on the server. - [textlink="Rule 3: Metagaming and Metacommunication" link="STMetagamingMetacommunication"] completely rewritten, with new violation examples and clarified faction knowledge, Xenomorph behavior, and voice-chat teaching allowances. - [textlink="Rule 4: ERP" link="STERP"] clarified that punishment depends on context and intent; minor IC profanity is not a violation. - [textlink="Rule 5: Bug Abuse and Unfair Play" link="STBuggingFoulPlay"] expanded violation list — now includes intentional glitching and visual bug abuse even “just for fun,” with clarified allowed exceptions. - [textlink="Rule 7: Play Your Role" link="STPlayYourRole"] fully reworked — role responsibility tiers and proper exit procedures added. This rule now defines core behavioral expectations and duty obligations within character roles. - [textlink="Rule 8: Character Names" link="STCharacterNames"] clarified naming requirements, with new restrictions on copying characters and CAPS-style nicknames.
The remaining rules have not been changed.
## Administrative decisions are final. - Rules are applied according to their intent. It is impossible to list every violation example; therefore enforcement depends on administrative interpretation of the rule’s meaning. - Administrators may resolve rule violations using in-game consequences for characters at their discretion. This does not apply to zero-tolerance rules. - You may submit an appeal to head staff if you believe a decision was incorrect.
## General Information Disabling, ignoring, or refusing to answer an admin’s ahelp will result in a ban that is only appealable. This is to prevent players from avoiding consequences. If you need to leave urgently, inform us — this will be taken into account when reviewing an appeal.
All rules below apply at all times, including between rounds.
## Core Rules These are the primary Space Stories - Marine Corps rules. Click on them for detailed information. - [textlink="Rule 0: Community Expectations" link="STCommunityExpectations"] - [textlink="Rule 1: Grief" link="STGrief"] - [textlink="Rule 2: Roleplay" link="STRolePlay"] - [textlink="Rule 3: Metagaming and Metacommunication" link="STMetagamingMetacommunication"] - [textlink="Rule 4: ERP" link="STERP"] - [textlink="Rule 5: Bug Abuse and Unfair Play" link="STBuggingFoulPlay"] - [textlink="Rule 6: Ban Evasion and Multi-Accounts" link="STBypassBlockingMultiAccounts"] - [textlink="Rule 7: Play Your Role" link="STPlayYourRole"] - [textlink="Rule 8: Character Names" link="STCharacterNames"] - [textlink="Rule 9: Use of Lethal Force" link="STUseOfLethalForce"] - [textlink="Rule 10: Military Law and Mutinies" link="STMilitaryLawMutinies"]
